nibbematiko.meaning: not disagreeing; in accordance, free from doubt or dissent
nibbematiko.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular (nir + vimati + ika)
breakdown: Prefix: without, free from. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)

nibbidāya.meaning: for weariness; for disgust with worldly life; for turning away
nibbidāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, dative singular of nibbidā
breakdown: Prefix: out, away. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To find, experience; nibbidā is weariness, disenchantment, disgust with worldly life (from nibbijjati).
Dative singular ending: "for the sake of".

nibbindati.meaning: is disgusted; becomes wearied of, turns away from
breakdown: Prefix: out, away. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To find, experience; with nir-, to become weary of, disenchanted with.

nibbutiṁ.meaning: peace; quenching, the bliss of extinguished craving
nibbutiṁ.gram: feminine abstract noun, accusative singular of nibbuti (from nibbāti, √vā)
breakdown: Prefix: out. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation.)
To blow; nibbuti, like nibbāna, is the "blowing out" or quenching, hence peace.

nibbāhitabbo.meaning: to be pulled out; to be removed
nibbāhitabbo.gram: future passive participle of nibbāhati (ni + √vah), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Prefix: out. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To carry; nibbāhati means to pull out, remove.

nibbāna.meaning: the ultimate liberation; the highest well-being; final freedom from suffering
nibbāna.gram: neuter noun (nir + √vā + ana, literally "blowing out, quenching")
breakdown: Prefix: out, away. (The doubled bb arises by assimilation of nir + v.)
To blow; nibbāna is literally the "blowing out" or quenching of the fires of craving, aversion and ignorance.
nibbāna.goenka: In the tradition nibbāna is introduced as the ultimate benefit and the highest well-being — final freedom from suffering. The canonical formula cited in the study material defines it through the ending of craving: taṇhākkhayo virāgo nibbāna, "annihilation of craving, fading away, nibbāna", and taṇhākkhayo nirodho nibbāna, "annihilation of craving, cessation, nibbāna". It is the third and final stage of the path's beauty: having walked the path of sīla, samādhi and paññā, the meditator ends "by realizing the fruit of nibbāna" — an experience to be realized directly, not a concept to be believed. The Pāli term is kept; "nirvana" (the Sanskrit form) is not used in tradition material.

nibbānapadābhiniropanaṁ.meaning: fixing the mind upon the path to nibbāna; application to the state of nibbāna
nibbānapadābhiniropanaṁ.summary: This is a compound meaning "nibbāna + path/state + fixing, application".
breakdown: The ultimate liberation, final freedom from suffering.
Fixing upon, applying, implanting (abhi + ni + √ruh, causative). (The long ā at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

nibbānasacchikiriyā.meaning: the realization of nibbāna; experiencing nibbāna for oneself
nibbānasacchikiriyā.summary: This is a compound meaning "nibbāna + realization, direct experience".
breakdown: The ultimate liberation, final freedom from suffering.
Realization, making real for oneself, direct experience (sacchi- "with one's own eyes" + kiriyā "doing").
nibbānasacchikiriyā.goenka: The term appears in the Maṅgalasutta among the highest welfares: nibbānasacchikiriyā, the realization of nibbāna. In the tradition this realization is the culmination of the path — nibbāna is not an object of belief or speculation but something the meditator experiences directly, by walking the path of sīla, samādhi and paññā to its end.

breakdown: To discern, decide; nicchaya is firm decision, resolution.
Prefix: out, forth. (The doubled dd is assimilation.)
To point out, show; niddisati means to declare, describe.

niddāsīlī.meaning: given to sleep; slothful, having the habit of drowsiness
breakdown: Having the habit or character of (from sīla "habit, conduct").

nidhānavatiṁ.gram: adjective, feminine accusative singular of nidhānavant
breakdown: Treasure, something stored away (ni + √dhā "to put down, deposit").

nidhīnaṁva.gram: sandhi fusion: masculine noun, genitive plural + comparison particle (nidhīnaṁ + va)

nidānāni.gram: neuter noun, nominative/accusative plural of nidāna
breakdown: To bind; nidāna is literally "tying down", hence ground, cause, origin.
To go; nigama is a place people resort to, a market town.

nigamo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular of nigama
breakdown: To go; nigama is a place people resort to, a market town.

niggacchati.meaning: proceeds from; goes out, comes forth
breakdown: Prefix: out. (The doubled gg arises by assimilation.)

niggahaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular (from niggaṇhāti, ni + √gah)
breakdown: Prefix: down, against. (The doubled gg arises by assimilation.)
To seize; niggaha is "holding down", hence censure, rebuke, refutation.

niggayhavādiṁ.meaning: one who speaks censuring; a speaker of rebuke (one who admonishes)
niggayhavādiṁ.gram: adjective, compound, accusative singular of niggayhavādī
breakdown: One who speaks, a speaker of (from vāda "speech").

nihita.meaning: laid down; put aside, deposited; set up, applied
breakdown: To put, place; the past participle nihita means laid down, deposited.

nihitadaṇḍo.meaning: one who has laid down the stick; having renounced violence
breakdown: Stick, rod; by extension punishment, violence.

nihitasattho.meaning: one who has laid down the sword; having renounced weapons

nihīyati.meaning: perishes; goes to waste, is ruined
nihīyati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, passive of ni + √hā
breakdown: To abandon, diminish; the passive nihīyati means to be brought low, perish.

nijigīsanatā.gram: feminine abstract noun (from nijigīsana, desiderative of √ji)
breakdown: Desiderative stem of √ji "to win": wanting to gain.
Abstract noun suffix: the state of hankering after gain.

nijjhāpetha.meaning: may you give to understand; cause to see, convince
nijjhāpetha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural imperative/optative, causative of nijjhāyati (ni + √jhā)
breakdown: Prefix: down, into. (The doubled jj arises by assimilation.)
To contemplate, meditate; the causative nijjhāpeti means to make someone see, to give to understand.

nijjiṇṇā.gram: past participle of nijjīrati (nir + √jīr), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Prefix: out, away. (The doubled jj arises by assimilation.)
To decay, wear out; the past participle nijjiṇṇa means worn away, destroyed.

nikkhepo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (from nikkhipati, ni + √khip)
breakdown: Prefix: down, away. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To throw; nikkhepa is the act of putting down, discarding.

nikkhipitvā.meaning: having put aside; having laid down
breakdown: To put down, lay aside (ni + √khip "to throw").

nikkhittaṁ.meaning: thrown down; put down, laid aside
nikkhittaṁ.gram: past participle of nikkhipati (ni + √khip), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Prefix: down, away. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To throw; the past participle nikkhitta means thrown down, deposited.
Prefix: down. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To bend, turn over; nikkujjita means overturned, upside down.

nikkujjitaṁ.meaning: overturned; fallen down, lying upside down
nikkujjitaṁ.gram: past participle of nikkujjeti, neuter/accusative singular

nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya….meaning: as if one were to set upright what was overturned
nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya….summary: This is the opening of the stock simile spoken by listeners gladdened by the Buddha's teaching.
breakdown: What is overturned, lying upside down (past participle of nikkujjeti).
Would set upright (optative of ukkujjeti "to turn up").

nikkāmino.meaning: those free from craving; desireless ones
nikkāmino.gram: adjective, masculine nominative plural of nikkāmī
breakdown: Prefix: without, free from. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
Possessive adjectival suffix: characterized by (here, by the absence of) desire.

nikubbetha.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative, middle voice of nikubbati (ni + √kar)
breakdown: Prefix: down, towards (here with a sense of doing someone down).
To do, make; nikubbati means to deceive, cheat.

nikāmalābhī.meaning: obtaining at will; one who gains easily and voluntarily
breakdown: Willingness, one's own desire; at will, voluntarily.
One who attains, a gainer (from lābha "gain").
To desire; nikāmeti means to desire, strive after.
nikāmeti.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss reads "not strive after", which reflects a negated occurrence (na nikāmeti) in the source passage; the verb itself means to strive after, desire.

nimantitassa.meaning: of one who has been invited
nimantitassa.gram: past participle of nimanteti (ni + √mant), genitive/dative singular

nimbabījaṁ.meaning: neem seed; seed of the neem tree
breakdown: The neem tree, known for its bitterness.

nimittaggāhī.meaning: grasping at signs; taking up the outward appearance (of sense objects)
breakdown: Taking, seizing, receiving (from √gah; the doubled gg is compound sandhi doubling).
nimittaggāhī.goenka: The term appears in the teaching on sense restraint (indriyasaṁvara): when seeing a form or hearing a sound, the practitioner does not become nimittaggāhī, one who grasps at the sign or outward appearance — the first step by which craving and aversion arise at the sense doors.

nimuggaposīni.meaning: immersed and nourished; sunk in and fed upon
breakdown: Immersed, sunk (past participle of nimujjati "to sink down").
To blame, reproach; nindā is blame, fault-finding.
Going (from √gam); a river is "that which goes to the low ground".

nipako.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular of nipaka

nippalāpāyaṁ.meaning: this one free from chatter; without prattle is this
breakdown: Prefix: without, free from. (The doubled pp arises by assimilation.)
This (nominative singular). (Fused with the preceding vowel.)

nippesikatā.meaning: trickery; deception, jugglery (an improper way of seeking gains)
breakdown: A trickster, one who wheedles or extorts (nir + √pis).
Abstract noun suffix: the practice of trickery.
